task: Find the top game listed in the Steam Deck's top-played list over the past year. Then, browse reviews for that game from players who have played over 100 hours and primarily use a Steam Deck.
final_result_response:
I have gathered all the information needed. Let me submit the final result.

The **#1 top-played game on Steam Deck over the past year** (Mar 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026) is **Stardew Valley**.

When filtering its reviews by players with **over 100 hours of playtime** who **played mostly on Steam Deck**, there are **755 reviews**, all rated **Overwhelmingly Positive**. Every single visible review recommends the game. Here's a summary of what these dedicated Steam Deck players said:

**Most Helpful Reviews (Past 30 Days):**
- **Soup Can Sam** (414.5 hrs): "Probably the GOAT! Get yourself the Stardew Valley Expanded mod if you've already sunk a lifetime into the game for even more incredible content."
- **Paragade** (160.6 hrs): "One of the best cozy and farm sim games in existence. That is as true when I first bought 10 years ago as it is today. Continues to receive updates from an involved developer, and the game enjoys a massive catalogue of amazing mods."
- **LakerFaker** (140.5 hrs): "One of the greatest games of all time. A true labor of love that will keep you coming back for more. With over 300 hours on the Switch in addition to my Steam playtime, it's always a good time to start a new farm."
- **Finch** (164.6 hrs): "This game is a go-to for me when I need something low pressure to distract myself with. You get to do everything at your own pace and there's no wrong way to play it."
- **Go** (367.6 hrs): "I really liked the love the creator put in the game… Perfect casual game to play with friends or in couple."

**Recently Posted:**
- **valhallenthehuman** (421.4 hrs): "I picked up Stardew Valley on my steam deck. It has been my saving grace during a horrible period of my life. It's incredibly cozy, engaging and beautifully crafted."
- **Supernova** (177.2 hrs): "My favorite go-to cozy game, its a masterpiece."
- **UnderAParsec** (311.3 hrs): "One of the greatest games of all time."
- **Sir Slaughters** (289.1 hrs): "A wonderful wholesome game."
- **Goose** (121.4 hrs): "Big game, good content."
- **CurlyChaos25** (122.9 hrs): "Amazing."
thoughts: I'll help you find the top game on Steam Deck's top-played list and browse its reviews. Let me start by navigating to the Steam store.
The Steam store has loaded. I need to find the Steam Deck's top-played list. Let me navigate to the Steam Deck section. I'll look under the Hardware menu first.
I can see the Hardware dropdown menu with "Steam Deck" option and "Great on Deck" link. Let me click on "Great on Deck" which should have the top-played games list.
This is the "Great on Deck" page showing featured games, but I need to find the specific "top-played" list. Let me scroll down to see if there's a top-played section.

I can see the "STEAM DECK TOP PLAYED GAMES" section with games like Disney Dreamlight Valley, Fallout 4, Resident Evil 4, and Palworld. But I need to see the full list with a "past year" filter. Let me click "View All" to see the complete list.

I can see the "STEAM DECK TOP PLAYED" page showing the top 100 played games. Currently it's set to "past week". I need to change this to "past year". Let me click on the dropdown to change the time period.
I can see the dropdown with three options: "past week", "past month", and "past year". Let me click on "past year".

Now I can see the Steam Deck Top Played list filtered by "past year" (Mar 1, 2025 - Feb 28, 2026). The #1 game is **Stardew Valley** at $14.99. Now I need to navigate to Stardew Valley's store page to browse its reviews. Let me click on it.

I'm on the Stardew Valley store page. I can see it has "Overwhelmingly Positive" reviews (378,275 English reviews). Now I need to scroll down to find the reviews section where I can filter by playtime and Steam Deck usage.
I can see the game page with purchase options and features. I need to scroll down further to find the reviews section.
I can see the Reviews section and Steam Deck Compatibility (Verified). I need to scroll down further to find the user reviews section where I can apply filters for playtime and Steam Deck usage.
I can see the "About This Game" section. I need to keep scrolling down to find the user reviews section with filters.
I see the system requirements and "More Like This" section. The user reviews section should be further down. Let me continue scrolling.
